title: Inside the Mind of a Hacker
body:
Report
Inside the Mind of a Hacker
Download Report
The preemptive shift in modern hacking
Inside the Mind of a Hacker
reveals how today’s hackers actually think and work—and why understanding those shifts is critical for staying ahead of real-world risk.
Hacking isn’t static. Motivations change. Collaboration is increasing. AI is reshaping how vulnerabilities are found. This report breaks down those changes and shows how security teams can respond more effectively right now.
What’s inside:
What motivates hackers and how to tailor your program to appeal to more hackers
Why more hackers are hacking in teams and how that benefits organizations
The top ways hackers are using AI to preemptively discover vulnerabilities
Download the Report
What are hackers finding?
71%
have found a new vulnerability in the past 12 months
85%
believe reporting a critical vulnerability is more important than making money from it
65%
have chosen not to disclose a vulnerability because there was no clear pathway to report it
How do hackers work?
61%
find more critical vulnerabilities when hacking in teams
82%
use AI as part of their hacking workflow
74%
believe AI has increased the value of hacking
Who are hackers?
92%
are 34 years old or younger
69%
graduated from college
81%
work full-time in a security-related field
“Security is a race where the finish line keeps moving forward and the only way to stay ahead is to run faster than everyone else. By utilizing AI, the pack not only gets ‘smarter’ but also quicker, running on a relentless 24/7 schedule.”
– OxMoose
